description_long: This object is a long, metallic knife or sword with a straight, flat blade featuring a series of evenly spaced perforations along its length. The blade appears to be made from a single piece of metal, likely steel, and has a utilitarian, industrial appearance. The handle is integrated into the same metal form as the blade, lacking any distinct grip or ergonomic shaping, and is simply an extension of the blade's material. The blade's edge is straight and unsharpened, with no visible serration or curvature, and the overall profile is rectangular. There are no visible decorative elements, engravings, or embellishments on the surface, giving it a purely functional look. The finish is matte and slightly reflective, with a hard, smooth surface typical of galvanized or stainless steel.
description: A long, perforated, straight-edged metallic knife or sword with an industrial, utilitarian design and no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: The object is seen from an angled end, highlighting the perforated blade and the flat, continuous handle.
description_view_1: A side profile showing the full length of the straight, perforated blade and the uniform width of the object.
description_view_2: An opposite angled end view, again emphasizing the perforations and the flat, unadorned handle.
description_view_3: A top-down view displaying the broad, flat surface of the blade and the regular pattern of holes.
attribute_handle_design: integrated, straight, flat, metallic, no grip texture
